Fix the following issue in daxini.xyz: fix: defer SECURITY_SECRET check to runtime to stop boot crash
Details: The top-level IIFE in sessionToken.js was throwing
'[FATAL] SECURITY_SECRET env var is required in production'
at module initialisation time — before any request handler ran.
Because api/index.js imports this module at the top level, the
serverless function failed to boot on every invocation, returning
500 FUNCTION_INVOCATION_FAILED for all routes.

Review and apply PR #682 in decide.engine-tools: fix(engine): bound invocation state registry lifecycle
Details: ### Motivation
- Prevent unbounded memory growth caused by per-invocation keys being stored in the state registry with no delete path. 
- Preserve per-invocation trace isolation while adding a deterministic cleanup lifecycle so long-running engine processes do not leak memory. 
- Add defensive safety layers (TTL + bounded capacity) to remove orphaned entries and keep registry size predictable. 

### Description
- Reworked `core/state-registry.js` to expose an explicit API (`set`, `get`, `delete`, `cleanupExpired`, `size`) and store entries with metadata `{ value, createdAt }`. 
- Added TTL ...

<|user|>
Review and apply PR #680 in decide.engine-tools: fix(engine): scope agent lifecycle failures to matching scheduler tasks on top of #678
Details: ### Motivation
- The runtime `step()` returns an aggregate report for all scheduled tasks, so treating any `report.failed > 0` as a lifecycle failure caused unrelated scheduled task errors to incorrectly fail agent runs and pollute traces. 
- Lifecycle failure detection must be scoped to the exact lifecycle task IDs (`agent:init:*`, `agent:run:*`, `agent:dispose:*`) so only the agent's own scheduled tasks can mark its run as failed. 
- Traces must reflect true agent failures (init/run/dispose) while ignoring unrelated background scheduler failures that occur in the same tick.

### Descripti...

Review and apply PR #679 in decide.engine-tools: fix(engine): reset agent lifecycle per invocation and handle scheduled run failures
Details: ### Motivation

- Prevent repeated `runAgent` calls from being blocked by a previous terminal state (e.g., `COMPLETED`) that was keyed to the agent id.  
- Ensure lifecycle traces reflect true outcomes when scheduled tasks fail instead of always recording `complete`.  
- Improve debugging by surfacing the failing flow id with thrown errors to make failing-run traces addressable.

### Description

- Scope state-machine transitions to a per-invocation key (`${agentId}:${flowId}`) in `core/agent-manager.js` so each run starts from the initial state.  
- Inspect the scheduler tick `report` retu...

<|user|>
Review and apply PR #678 in decide.engine-tools: fix(engine): propagate scheduled agent lifecycle failures on top of #675
Details: ### Motivation
- `Scheduler.runTick` captures task exceptions into execution reports instead of re-throwing, so `AgentManager.runAgent` incorrectly assumed `runtime.step()` would throw on lifecycle failures and thus swallowed init/run/dispose errors. 
- Swallowed lifecycle failures caused incorrect trace outcomes and masked failures during agent runs, breaking failure isolation and trace accuracy. 
- The change ensures lifecycle errors observed in scheduler reports are propagated as run failures and recorded on the trace flow.

### Description
- Updated `core/agent-manager.js` `runAgent` to...

Review and apply PR #677 in decide.engine-tools: fix(engine): use propagated trace engine in PluginSystem.executePlugin
Details: ### Motivation
- Fix a P1 bug where `PluginSystem.executePlugin` opened spans on its own `TraceEngine` even when `traceContext.flowId` belonged to an external trace, causing `Unknown flow` and breaking cross-runtime trace propagation. 
- Ensure plugin execution gracefully participates in caller-created flows and records spans in the originating trace engine. 
- Add regression coverage to prevent regressions in trace propagation.

### Description
- Updated `core/plugin-system.js` to prefer the caller-provided trace engine from `traceContext.trace` and to use that engine for `startFlow`/`star...

<|user|>
Review and apply PR #151 in daxini.xyz: fix(vercel): harden api handler to prevent FUNCTION_INVOCATION_FAILED
Details: ### Motivation
- The `/api/*` rewrite funnels all API traffic to a single Vercel entrypoint (`api/index.js`), creating a single point of failure that produced `FUNCTION_INVOCATION_FAILED` (500) in production.  
- Improve observability and avoid runtime crashes by adding logs-based diagnostics and environment validation so missing/misconfigured env values do not cause uncaught exceptions.  
- Provide deterministic, fallback-safe responses for common cases (health GET and method fallbacks) while preserving the static-site deployment model and avoiding any framework or build-step changes.  

#...

<|user|>
Review and apply PR #150 in daxini.xyz: fix: replace tunnel proxy with stable Vercel-native API handler
Details: The thin-proxy implementation depended on a Cloudflare tunnel URL
(brain_config.js) that goes offline whenever the Mac Mini is
unreachable, producing 503/500 errors visible in production logs
for every request that hit /api/*.

Replace with a minimal, self-contained Vercel serverless handler
that returns 200 {"status":"ok","service":"daxini"} for GET and
405 for other methods — guaranteed to work without any external
dependency. The tunnel-based architecture can be reintroduced later
once the sovereign brain connection is reliable.

Review and apply PR #148 in daxini.xyz: fix: defer SECURITY_SECRET check to runtime to stop boot crash
Details: The top-level IIFE in sessionToken.js was throwing
'[FATAL] SECURITY_SECRET env var is required in production'
at module initialisation time — before any request handler ran.
Because api/index.js imports this module at the top level, the
serverless function failed to boot on every invocation, returning
500 FUNCTION_INVOCATION_FAILED for all routes.

Fix: replace the module-level constant with a lazy getSessionSecret()
function that reads the env var on first call.  The env-var guard still
fires (and throws) when SECURITY_SECRET is absent and a token operation
is actually attempted — all oth...

<|user|>
Review and apply PR #143 in daxini.xyz: fix(pwa): bump SW CACHE_VERSION to daxini-v3 (invalidate stale clients)
Details: ## Summary

Users on the PWA were still hitting `Local runtime timeout` after 30s — a bug from commit `ae62b6b` ("replace remote execute POST with local runtime bridge") that was already rolled back in PR #136 (`a283bcf`).

The rollback fixed `main`, but users with the PWA installed kept seeing it because their Service Worker had cached `zayvora-app.js` (and `zayvora.html`) under `daxini-v2` and the `cacheFirst` strategy never refetches.

## Fix

One-line bump: `daxini-v2` → `daxini-v3`. The SW `activate` handler then deletes all `daxini-v2*` caches and refetches from origin. `skipWaiting()...
<|assistant|>
